How to use Clothing Studio

Make a Roblox outfit in 5 easy steps. You can open this guide again any time with the ❓ How to use button at the top.

1Pick Shirt or Pants. Use the Shirt / Pants buttons at the bottom to choose what you're making.
2Choose a color. Click the color square at the top-left, or click any of the little colored squares next to it.
3Pick a tool and draw. On the left, choose Brush to draw, Fill to color a whole area, Text to add words, or Image to add a picture. Then draw on the white grid in the middle.
4The colored boxes are the parts of the clothing — front, back, arms and legs. Color inside the boxes. Your outfit shows up on the character on the right (drag it to spin).
5Export it. When you're happy, click ↓ Export Template at the bottom, then upload that picture to Roblox.

Oops — made a mistake?

↶ History — the list on the top-left shows everything you've done; click any step (or press Ctrl+Z) to go back
✕ Clear — wipe the selected layer clean

What the tools do

Pan — slide the whole canvas
Brush — draw freehand
Line — draw a straight line
Eraser — rub things out
Fill — color a whole area
Text — type words (Enter = new line, bold/italic/underline, align)
Stamps — stamp stars, hearts…
Picker — copy a color on screen
Replace — swap one color for another everywhere (one layer or all)
Image — upload pictures/logos to your saved library, then click one to place it (they're kept for next time)
Gradient — blend two colors
Pattern — fill with stripes, dots…
Move — slide something you made
Select — box an area to move/copy
Smudge — push colors around
Blur — soften an area

More handy buttons

🎨 Color picker — full HSV picker with color harmonies
▦ Pixel — hard pixels, no blur (1px brush = 1 pixel)
⇆ Mirror — flip a layer to the other side of the body
Layer opacity / blend — fade or blend each layer
⧉ ⬇ ▣ — duplicate, merge down, flatten layers

My Designs 💾

Your designs are saved in this browser on this device. Use ⤓ on a design to download a backup you can keep or move to another computer.

Shading Templates ✨

Start from a premade fabric shading template. It drops soft folds and depth onto a new layer above your design — paint your colours on the layers underneath, then fine-tune its opacity or blend mode in the Layers panel.

Your uploads are saved in this browser and shown below.

Choose a theme 🎨

Pick the look of the studio. Your choice is saved on this device and remembered next time.
